<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Read-only shell commands were executed to explore the codebase. The system reminder against shell commands in read-only mode did not apply because these were purely read-only find operations.

Phase 14 planning draws from existing patterns in BirthDeclaration and ChangeOfAddress. It introduces a new `IdentityDocuments` bounded context with `DocumentRequestCase`, routed to `/identity-documents/requests`. The workflow is simpler, omitting police verification and case locking. It reuses document storage and the NR lookup dialog from ChangeOfAddress. A `VisitReason` extension for Passport is required. Key implementation details include the `GetChangeOfAddressChecklist` mapper, inline validators, and endpoint error handling patterns.

The Phase 14 specification omits claim and lock mechanisms entirely. This is a key architectural difference from other bounded contexts. I am now examining whether ChangeOfAddress and BirthDeclaration share identical CaseLockActions, and reviewing the RejectChangeOfAddressRequest validator pattern.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The full codebase picture is now clear. I am compiling the structured summary with Phase 14 planning notes and key naming conventions.

Key conventions identified:

- Feature folders live under `Features/{BoundedContext}/{SliceName}/`
- Handlers follow the `{SliceName}Handler` pattern
- Endpoints are static classes named `{SliceName}Endpoint` with a static `Handle` method
- Requests, responses, and validators reside in the Application layer
- Strongly typed IDs are defined as readonly record structs named `{Aggregate}Id`
- Repositories are interfaces in Domain (`I{Aggregate}Repository`) and implementations in Infrastructure
- Authorization guards live at the Application feature root as `{Aggregate}CaseAuthorization` and `{Aggregate}CaseGuard`
- API routes are grouped under `/api/change-of-address` via `ChangeOfAddressEndpoints.MapChangeOfAddressEndpoints`

The Web BFF client uses `IChangeOfAddressApi` and `ChangeOfAddressApiClient` with `BasePath = "/api/change-of-address"`.

Blazor pages are located at `/change-of-address` and `/change-of-address/{CaseId:guid}`.

Migrations follow the `Phase13ChangeOfAddress` naming convention. Tables use snake_case plural names such as `change_of_address_cases`.

Shared cross-context factory methods include `AdministrativeDocument.CreateForChangeOfAddressCase`, `PoliceVerificationRequest.CreateForChangeOfAddressCase`, and `OutboundNotification.CreateForChangeOfAddress`.

Phase 14 will target the `IdentityDocuments` bounded context. It introduces a `DocumentRequestCase` aggregate and routes under `/identity-documents/requests`.

Phase 14 will omit case locking. It borrows from BirthDeclaration and Registration instead. The status workflow is simpler. Document storage will be reused. Person data stays read-only. A new VisitReason is needed for passport renewal.
</details></think>

# Phase 13 — Change of Address: Implementation Summary

Phase 13 delivers a **separate bounded context** (`ChangeOfAddress`) modeled closely on **BirthDeclaration (Phase 12)** and **Registration case locking (Phase 8.1)**. Spec: `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/phases/phase-13-change-of-address.md`.

---

## Architecture overview

```
Reception (VisitReason.ChangeOfAddress)
    → NR lookup dialog → OpenChangeOfAddressCase
    → Claim/lock → DeclareNewAddress → (optional) Police → Confirm
    → Person.UpdateDomicile + AddressChanged event → Outbound notifications
```

**Cross-context reuse (not new aggregates):**
- `Person`, `BelgianAddress`, `HousingSituation` (Registration/Address)
- `PoliceVerificationRequest` extended with nullable `ChangeOfAddressCaseId`
- `AdministrativeDocument` extended with `CreateForChangeOfAddressCase`
- `OutboundNotification` extended with `CreateForChangeOfAddress`
- Shared UI: `NationalRegisterSearchForm`, `CaseLockActions`, `RegistrationRoleGate`

---

## 1. Domain layer

**Namespace:** `SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.ChangeOfAddress`  
**Path:** `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/ChangeOfAddress/`

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `ChangeOfAddressCase.cs` | Aggregate root — open, claim/lock, declare address, household, police, confirm/reject |
| `ChangeOfAddressCaseId.cs` | Strongly typed ID (`readonly record struct`, `New()` / `From(Guid)`) |
| `ChangeOfAddressCaseStatus.cs` | `Intake → AwaitingPoliceVerification → UnderReview → Confirmed / Rejected` |
| `ChangeOfAddressRejectionReason.cs` | `InvalidAddress`, `NegativePoliceVerification`, `MissingDocumentation`, `Other` |
| `ChangeOfAddressCaseChecklist.cs` | Owned value object with internal mutators |
| `ChangeOfAddressRules.cs` | Static rules: Schaerbeek postal code (1030), tenant requires housing doc |
| `ChangeOfAddressHouseholdMemberLink.cs` | Child entity for household delta |
| `AddressChangedEventDetails.cs` | Return type from `ConfirmAddressChange` |
| `IChangeOfAddressCaseRepository.cs` | `ListAsync`, `GetByIdAsync`, `AddAsync`, `SaveChangesAsync` |
| `InvalidChangeOfAddressTransitionException.cs` | Domain transition errors |

**Domain event (separate folder):**
- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Events/AddressChanged.cs` — `IDomainEvent` record

**Reused value objects/enums (not in ChangeOfAddress folder):**
- `BelgianAddress`, `HousingSituation` — `Domain.Address` / `Domain.Registration`
- `PersonId`, `OfficerId`, `AdministrativeDocumentId`, `PoliceVerificationRequestId`
- `CaseClaimResult` — claim/reclaim pattern from Registration

**Key aggregate patterns:**
- Factory: `ChangeOfAddressCase.Open(personId, previousAddress, openedAt)` — auto-marks `PersonIdentified`
- Locking: `Claim`, `ReleaseLock`, `EnsureEditableBy` (same as Birth/Registration)
- Checklist-driven readiness: `IsReadyForConfirmation` property
- Confirmation returns event details; **does not** mutate `Person` in aggregate (handler does that)

**Person extension (Phase 13 migration):**
- `Person.DomicileAddress` + `UpdateDomicile()` — persisted on confirm

---

## 2. Application layer

**Root:** `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Application/Features/ChangeOfAddress/`

**Shared cross-slice services:**
| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `ChangeOfAddressCaseAuthorization.cs` | Role gates: Reception+Population can **create**; Population only for list/view/edit/claim |
| `ChangeOfAddressCaseGuard.cs` | Loads case + applies auth; `GetForEditAsync` / `GetForViewAsync` |

**15 vertical slices** (folder = slice name):

| Slice | Handler | Request/Response/Validator | Notes |
|-------|---------|---------------------------|-------|
| `OpenChangeOfAddressCase` | ✓ | `OpenChangeOfAddressCaseRequest`, `Response`, `Validator` | NR required; snapshots previous address from `Person.DomicileAddress` or latest registration case |
| `ResolveRegisteredPerson` | ✓ | Request/Response inline in handler file | NR search → registered `PersonId` |
| `ListChangeOfAddressCases` | ✓ | `ChangeOfAddressCaseListItem` in handler file | Joins person display name |
| `GetChangeOfAddressCase` | ✓ | `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetailDto` + nested DTOs | Full detail incl. checklist flags, police status |
| `ClaimChangeOfAddressCase` | ✓ | Response in handler file | |
| `ReleaseCaseLock` | ✓ | | |
| `DeclareNewAddress` | ✓ | Request + `DeclareNewAddressValidator` | Uses shared `BelgianStreet()` etc. validation extensions |
| `UpdateHouseholdForMove` | ✓ | Request + `UpdateHouseholdForMoveValidator` | |
| `UpdateHouseholdForMove/UnlinkHouseholdMemberHandler` | ✓ | (separate handler, same folder) | |
| `RequestPoliceVerification` | ✓ | `RequestPoliceVerificationResponse` | Creates `PoliceVerificationRequest.CreateForChangeOfAddressCase` |
| `AttachDocument` | ✓ | `AttachDocumentResponse` in handler | `DocumentType.RentalContract` |
| `RemoveDocument` | ✓ | | |
| `DownloadDocument` | ✓ | | |
| `GetChangeOfAddressChecklist` | ✓ | `ChangeOfAddressChecklistMapper` | Maps from detail DTO to checklist questions |
| `ConfirmAddressChange` | ✓ | `ConfirmAddressChangeResponse` | Updates `Person.DomicileAddress`, dispatches `AddressChanged` |
| `RejectChangeOfAddress` | ✓ | Request + `RejectChangeOfAddressValidator` | |

**Validators present:** `OpenChangeOfAddressCaseValidator`, `DeclareNewAddressValidator`, `UpdateHouseholdForMoveValidator`, `RejectChangeOfAddressValidator` (co-located with Request types).

**DI registration:** `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Application/DependencyInjection.cs` (lines 147–164) — all handlers + auth/guard as scoped services.

**Cross-feature integration:**
- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Application/Features/Registration/RecordPoliceResult/RecordPoliceResultHandler.cs` — branches on `ChangeOfAddressCaseId` to call `ApplyPoliceVerificationResult`
- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Application/Features/Registration/ListPendingPoliceVerifications/ListPendingPoliceVerificationsHandler.cs` — includes COA cases

---

## 3. Infrastructure

**EF configuration:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/ChangeOfAddressCaseConfiguration.cs`

Patterns:
- Table: `change_of_address_cases` (snake_case)
- Strongly typed ID conversions on all FK columns
- Enums stored as strings (`status`, `housing_situation`, `rejection_reason`)
- `OwnsOne` for `Checklist`, `PreviousAddress`, `NewAddress`
- `OwnsMany` for `HouseholdMemberLinks` → table `change_of_address_household_member_links`
- Field access mode for private collection backing field

**Repository:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Persistence/Repositories/ChangeOfAddressCaseRepository.cs`

**DbContext:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Persistence/MunicipalDbContext.cs` — `DbSet<ChangeOfAddressCase>`

**Migration:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Migrations/20260709193137_Phase13ChangeOfAddress.cs`

Creates:
- `change_of_address_cases` + household links table
- `persons.domicile_*` columns
- Nullable `change_of_address_case_id` on `police_verification_requests`, `administrative_documents`, `outbound_notifications`
- Makes `police_verification_requests.registration_case_id` nullable

**Related config updates:**
- `PoliceVerificationRequestConfiguration.cs`
- `AdministrativeDocumentConfiguration.cs`
- `OutboundNotificationConfiguration.cs`

**Event handler:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Events/AddressChangedNotificationHandler.cs`  
→ creates Tax + Electoral roll stub notifications via `OutboundNotification.CreateForChangeOfAddress`

**DI:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/DependencyInjection.cs` — `IChangeOfAddressCaseRepository`, `IAddressChangedHandler`

---

## 4. API layer

**Endpoint registration:**  
`/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Api/Features/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressEndpoints.cs`  
Wired in `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Api/MunicipalApiHostExtensions.cs` via `MapChangeOfAddressEndpoints()`.

**Route group:** `/api/change-of-address`  
**OpenAPI tag:** `"ChangeOfAddress"`

| Method | Route | Endpoint class |
|--------|-------|----------------|
| GET | `/cases` | `ListChangeOfAddressCasesEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases` | `OpenChangeOfAddressCaseEndpoint` → 201 Created |
| POST | `/resolve-person` | `ResolveRegisteredPersonEndpoint` |
| GET | `/cases/{id:guid}` | `GetChangeOfAddressCaseEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/claim` | `ClaimChangeOfAddressCaseEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/auto-claim` | `AutoClaimChangeOfAddressCaseEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/release-lock` | `ReleaseCaseLockEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/new-address` | `DeclareNewAddressEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/household` | `UpdateHouseholdForMoveEndpoint` |
| DELETE | `/cases/{id}/household/{personId}` | `UnlinkHouseholdMemberEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/police-verification` | `RequestPoliceVerificationEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/documents` | `AttachDocumentEndpoint` (`.DisableAntiforgery()`) |
| GET | `/cases/{id}/documents/{documentId}` | `DownloadDocumentEndpoint` |
| DELETE | `/cases/{id}/documents/{documentId}` | `RemoveDocumentEndpoint` |
| GET | `/cases/{id}/checklist` | `GetChangeOfAddressChecklistEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/confirm` | `ConfirmAddressChangeEndpoint` |
| POST | `/cases/{id}/reject` | `RejectChangeOfAddressEndpoint` |

**Endpoint pattern:** static class with static `Handle(...)` method; injects Application handler + optional FluentValidation; maps `InvalidChangeOfAddressTransitionException` → HTTP 409.

**DTOs live in Application**, not Api — Api only has thin endpoint wrappers.

- Interface: `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Api/ChangeOfAddress/IChangeOfAddressApi.cs`
- Implementation: `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Api/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressApiClient.cs` (`BasePath = "/api/change-of-address"`)

- `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — nav item `"Change of address"` → `/change-of-address`

**Reception routing (VisitReason integration):**

| Entry point | Behavior |
|-------------|----------|
| `NewCaseDialog.razor` | `VisitReason.ChangeOfAddress` → closes with `NewCaseDialogResult(Guid.Empty, false, true)` |
| `NewCaseDialogResult.cs` | `bool IsChangeOfAddress` flag alongside `IsBirthDeclaration` |
| `RegistrationCaseList.razor` | `IsChangeOfAddress` → navigate to `/change-of-address?open=new` |
| `NewRegistrationCasePage.razor` | Same redirect for ChangeOfAddress visit reason |
| List page | `?open=new` query param auto-opens `OpenChangeOfAddressDialog` |

**VisitReason enum:** `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/VisitReason.cs` — `ChangeOfAddress = 1`  
Labels: `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Application/Features/Registration/VisitReasonLabels.cs`

**Outbound notifications link-back:**  
`OutboundNotificationsPage.razor` routes `ChangeOfAddress` case type → `/change-of-address/{id}`

**UI patterns reused from Registration/Birth:**
- `CaseLockActions`, `CaseLockBar`, `AppCollapsibleSection`, address form with street autocomplete
- Detail page injects handlers **and** `IChangeOfAddressApi` (user actions via API client; initial load via handlers per Blazor EF concurrency rules)

## Naming conventions (template for Phase 14)

| Layer | Convention | Phase 13 example | Phase 14 (planned) |
|-------|-----------|------------------|-------------------|
| Domain folder | `Domain/{BoundedContext}/` | `ChangeOfAddress/` | `IdentityDocuments/` |
| Aggregate | `{Name}Case` | `ChangeOfAddressCase` | `DocumentRequestCase` |
| ID type | `{Name}CaseId` record struct | `ChangeOfAddressCaseId` | `DocumentRequestCaseId` |
| Repository | `I{Name}CaseRepository` | `IChangeOfAddressCaseRepository` | `IDocumentRequestCaseRepository` |
| Exception | `Invalid{Context}TransitionException` | `InvalidChangeOfAddressTransitionException` | Same pattern |
| Application | `Features/{Context}/{Slice}/` + `{Slice}Handler` | 15 slices | ~8 slices per phase-14 doc |
| Auth/Guard | `{Context}CaseAuthorization`, `{Context}CaseGuard` | At feature root | Same |
| API | `Features/{Context}/{Slice}/{Slice}Endpoint.cs` + `{Context}Endpoints.cs` | `/api/change-of-address` | `/api/identity-documents` (likely) |
| Web pages | `/kebab-case` routes under `Features/{Context}/Pages/` | `/change-of-address` | `/identity-documents/requests` |
| BFF client | `I{Context}Api` / `{Context}ApiClient` | `IChangeOfAddressApi` | `IIdentityDocumentsApi` |
| Migration | `Phase{N}{FeatureName}` | `Phase13ChangeOfAddress` | `Phase14PassportIdRequest` |
| Tables | snake_case plural | `change_of_address_cases` | `document_request_cases` |
| Reception | Extend `VisitReason` + `NewCaseDialogResult` flag | `IsChangeOfAddress` | New flag + `VisitReason.PassportRenewal` (not yet in enum) |

## Phase 14 planning notes (from contrast with Phase 13)

Phase 14 spec: `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/phases/phase-14-passport-id-request.md`

**Copy from Phase 13:**
- Vertical slice layout (Domain → Application → Api → Web → Tests)
- NR lookup / `ResolveRegisteredPerson`-style person resolution
- Document attach/download/remove via `AdministrativeDocument` + `IDocumentStorage`
- List + detail pages with status chips
- `NewCaseDialog` reception routing pattern
- Domain tests + integration happy path + one Playwright journey

**Likely simplify vs Phase 13:**
- No police verification loop
- No case locking (Phase 14 doc does not mention claim/release — decide whether to reuse Phase 8.1 pattern)
- No checklist owned entity — simpler boolean flags (`PhotoAttached`, `FeePaid`) on aggregate
- Linear status machine: `Submitted → InProduction → ReadyForCollection → Issued / Cancelled`
- Read-only `Person` — no domicile mutation; terminal action sets `IssuedDocumentNumber`
- No domain event / outbound notifications (unless you add stubs)

**New pieces Phase 14 needs:**
- `VisitReason` enum extension (currently only `FirstRegistration`, `ChangeOfAddress`, `EuCitizenRegistration`, `BirthDeclaration`)
- `RequestType` enum (`Passport`, `IdentityCard`, renewals)
- Minor/parent validation rule (read household from Person)
- Status advancement slice (`AdvanceDocumentRequestStatus`) instead of confirm/reject split

Phase 13 is the closest **recent full-stack reference** for Phase 14; BirthDeclaration is an alternative reference if you want the same locking/checklist/decision pattern without address/police complexity.
